name: chr7_q11_23_Williams_Beuren_flank
description: Classic 1.4 Mb Williams-Beuren syndrome interval flanked by tripartite direct-orientation LCR clusters (~99.6% identity, ~320 kb) of GTF2I/GTF2IRD1/NCF1/POM121/STAG3L paralogs. The most extensively characterized NAHR locus; recurrent deletions cause WBS, reciprocal duplications cause 7q11.23 dup syndrome with ASD/speech delay.

architecture.internal_structure: Williams-Beuren critical region: ELN, LIMK1, GTF2I, GTF2IRD1, BAZ1B core, flanked by tripartite LCR clusters (A/B/C copies in centromeric, medial, telomeric blocks)
